title: Identify all missense variants, including combined VEP annotations
description: A 20-row VEP-style TSV where some rows have combined consequence annotations like 'missense_variant&splice_region_variant'. The task is to identify every variant that IS a missense (whether on its own or in combination with another annotation). Tests whether the agent understands the '&'-separated VEP consequence convention.
task_prompt: You are given a TSV file at vep_combined_annotations.tsv in your working directory. It has columns: variant_id, gene, hgvs_c, consequence, impact. The consequence column may contain a single SO term (e.g. 'missense_variant') OR multiple SO terms joined with '&' (e.g. 'missense_variant&splice_region_variant') - the latter means both consequences apply to that variant. Identify every variant whose consequence list INCLUDES 'missense_variant' (either alone or as part of an '&'-separated list). Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with this exact schema: {"missense_variants": ["<variant_id>", ...]}. Use the variant_id strings verbatim. Order does not matter.

inputs.description: 20 annotated variants, two with combined VEP annotations

notes: 8 missense variants total: 6 'missense_variant' alone + 2 'missense_variant&splice_region_variant' combined. The prompt explicitly explains the '&' convention so the task is fair; the eval tests whether the model implements the convention correctly. A strict-equality filter (consequence == 'missense_variant') yields 6 - missing the two combined rows - Jaccard 6/8 = 0.75. Strict set_match (no Jaccard threshold) so missing both gives FAIL outright.
